title: Prestonia contorta
taxonomy: Prestonia contorta belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This plant is classified as a type of evergreen shrub. Prestonia contorta is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is also known as the "cassava shrub" due to its resemblance to the cassava plant. The species is further classified within the genus Prestonia, which consists of several species of tropical plants.
uses: Prestonia contorta is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Prestonia contorta, also known as "yerba de pasmo," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as its antimalarial and antibacterial properties. Prestonia contorta contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
